programme.title: The Haunted
programme.desc: How the alleged spirit of a man who was murdered in the basement of Al and Marias restaurant is terrorising their pet dogs. Stories of paranormal events

programme.desc: Terri helps determine if Caballe the rhino is pregnant, while Robert matchmakes the zoos Aldabra giant tortoises

programme.title: My Cat from Hell

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy investigates the behaviour of cat Einstein, which is mild until it sees fellow feline Izzy - and then goes on the attack. Plus, an expectant couple worry about their pets mood swings

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: A new cadet almost gets run down in Texas, an armed trespasser is reported in Beaumont and wardens seek illegal gill nets on the Rio Grande

programme.desc: Dr Jeff and his team battle to save a puppy with broken ribs and internal bleeding. Meanwhile, Dr Baier attempts surgery he has never done before to treat a familys beloved rat

programme.desc: Pete Nelson and his gang ride out to build a double-decker hideout in Texas Hill Country, featuring a saloon-style first floor

programme.desc: A dog needs knee surgery, meaning Dr Jeff may be its only hope. Dr Nichols and Dr Petra team up to fix a cats broken elbow

programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps newly-married couple John and Samantha. The pair are having trouble taming the latters cat, Puma, which seems resentful of John in particular

programme.desc: Pete Nelson heads to the Bella Rose plantation in Virginia, where he builds a luxurious Thomas Jefferson-inspired treehouse complete with colonial columns

programme.desc: A bird expert reveals how a barn owl catches prey it cannot even see. Plus, what makes octopuses masters of disguise

programme.title: Mosquito
programme.desc: Leading scientists share controversial means of dealing with mosquitos, which pose a unique threat as the single greatest agent of death in modern human history

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson visits Alnmouth, Northumberland, which was founded in the mid-12th century after the barons of nearby Alnwick obtained a charter from King John to build a new village. It had a market and a small port and the village flourished - so much so that by the mid-1700s, Alnmouths grain export started to play an integral role in feeding the growing English population

programme.title: Wainwright Walks: Coast to Coast
programme.desc: A third of the way through Alfred Wainwrights route across northern England, Julia Bradbury reaches the Eden Valley. The bustling town of Kirkby Stephen marks the beginning of her climb over the Pennines, but wet weather makes for a challenging journey

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett examines the Normans expansion toward southern Europe and the Middle East in the 11th century. Southern Italy and Sicily were conquered and a prosperous kingdom flourished on the island, which became an important centre of medieval culture and an example of harmony between religions. However, Pope Urban IIs call for holy war against the Muslims, to win Jerusalem back, led to the Normans embarking on the First Crusade

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 11 June 1999, featuring Backstreet Boys, 21st Century Girls, Another Level, The Chemical Brothers, Cher, Feeder, Red Hot Chili Peppers and Baz Luhrmann

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 18 June 1999, featuring The Wiseguys, Shania Twain, Madonna, Cartoons, Tatyana Ali, Cher, Lauryn Hill and S Club 7

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Presenters Tony Blackburn and Gary Davies introduce an edition from March 17 1983, featuring Bananarama, the Style Council, Ultravox, Joan Armatrading, Bonnie Tyler and Bucks Fizz

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Janice Long and Simon Bates present an edition first aired on 20 March 1986, featuring performances by Jim Diamond, Pet Shop Boys, Cliff Richard and the Young Ones, the Real Thing, Diana Ross and Mr Mister

programme.title: Neil Sedaka Says: All You Need Is the Music
programme.desc: A one-man show from 1983 in which the late American singer-songwriter performs a diverse range of numbers from his catalogue, from the rock n roll era to a more lyrical age. Songs include Solitaire, Bad Blood, The Hungry Years, Laughter in the Rain and New York City Blues

programme.title: Legends
programme.sub-title: Neil Sedaka: Thats When the Music Takes Me
programme.desc: Documentary profiling songwriter and performer Neil Sedaka, the man behind hits like Oh! Carol, (Is This the Way to) Amarillo and Solitaire. With input from Tony Christie, Connie Francis, Graham Gouldman and Jay Siegel

programme.title: Neil Sedaka: In Concert
programme.desc: A 1975 performance by the singer-songwriter, featuring Love Will Keep Us Together, Laughter in the Rain, The Queen of 1964 and Breaking Up Is Hard to Do

programme.title: Piano Greats at the BBC
programme.desc: A collection of songs from the kings and queens of the keyboards, whose performances have captivated audiences over the decades across a selection of the BBCs best-loved music programmes. From piano pioneers like Little Richard, Nina Simone and Ray Charles, who showed the world that the new sounds of pop and rock didnt just revolve around guitars, right through to modern maestros like Elton John, Lady Gaga and Jools Holland

programme.title: Neil Sedaka Says: All You Need Is the Music
programme.desc: A one-man show from 1983 in which the late American singer-songwriter performs a diverse range of numbers from his catalogue, from the rock n roll era to a more lyrical age. Songs include Solitaire, Bad Blood, The Hungry Years, Laughter in the Rain and New York City Blues

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 11 June 1999, featuring Backstreet Boys, 21st Century Girls, Another Level, The Chemical Brothers, Cher, Feeder, Red Hot Chili Peppers and Baz Luhrmann

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 18 June 1999, featuring The Wiseguys, Shania Twain, Madonna, Cartoons, Tatyana Ali, Cher, Lauryn Hill and S Club 7

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Presenters Tony Blackburn and Gary Davies introduce an edition from March 17 1983, featuring Bananarama, the Style Council, Ultravox, Joan Armatrading, Bonnie Tyler and Bucks Fizz

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Janice Long and Simon Bates present an edition first aired on 20 March 1986, featuring performances by Jim Diamond, Pet Shop Boys, Cliff Richard and the Young Ones, the Real Thing, Diana Ross and Mr Mister

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary capturing the beauty of butterflies close up, and revealing how they inspire the images of street artist Nick Walker and the performances of burlesque dancer Vicky Butterfly. The programme also raises the question of whether a passion for these elegant insects could help people preserve much-loved landscapes. Narrated by Imelda Staunton

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: A 1977 edition of the old-time music hall programme, chaired by Leonard Sachs. Mike Reid, the Beverley Sisters, Valerie Monese, Graham Clark and members of the Players Theatre, London, entertain an audience from the stage of Leeds City Varieties

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth disguises herself as a victim of domestic abuse in an attempt to find out who is threatening the future of a womens refuge. Hettys quest to find the leak opens her eyes to the terrifying realities of spousal abuse, and results in a gripping conclusion as she leads the women in defending their sanctuary against a violent motorcycle gang

programme.title: La Chimera (2023)
programme.desc: Just out of jail, English archaeologist Arthur reconnects with his wayward crew of accomplices, a happy-go-lucky collective of grave-robbers who survive by looting Etruscan tombs and fencing the ancient treasures they dig up. Comedy adventure, starring Josh OConnor, Carol Duarte, Vincenzo Nemolato and Isabella Rossellini. In Italian, English, French and Brazilian Portuguese

programme.title: Parkinson
programme.desc: Michael Parkinson interviews Alistair Cooke, the journalist and broadcaster best known for his long-running radio programme Letter from America. First broadcast in 1980

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Daisy tries to encourage Onslow to notice her by picking up a toyboy in the hope of making him jealous. Meanwhile, Hyacinth forces Elizabeth and Richard to help her organise a social occasion at the church hall. Patricia Routledge and Judy Cornwell star

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy is sent to speak to the father of the boy bullying his godson - and discovers bad behaviour runs in the family

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth disguises herself as a victim of domestic abuse in an attempt to find out who is threatening the future of a womens refuge. Hettys quest to find the leak opens her eyes to the terrifying realities of spousal abuse, and results in a gripping conclusion as she leads the women in defending their sanctuary against a violent motorcycle gang

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: A 1977 edition of the old-time music hall programme, chaired by Leonard Sachs. Mike Reid, the Beverley Sisters, Valerie Monese, Graham Clark and members of the Players Theatre, London, entertain an audience from the stage of Leeds City Varieties

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary capturing the beauty of butterflies close up, and revealing how they inspire the images of street artist Nick Walker and the performances of burlesque dancer Vicky Butterfly. The programme also raises the question of whether a passion for these elegant insects could help people preserve much-loved landscapes. Narrated by Imelda Staunton

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: A panel of BBC journalists hosts a weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering the usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Crimewatch Live
programme.desc: How a bookkeeper took nearly £900,000 from an elderly couple she had befriended. Police appeal for help identifying a man they believe was killed in a forest in north Wales

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the development progress of properties in Carlisle, Rosyth in Fife and Shepherds Bush, west London

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles looks at some of the best medically themed purchases from the Bargain Hunt archives, including a collection of gruesome slides and a vintage anatomy poster

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Father investigates when a Hollywood starlet is murdered while filming an advert for washing powder. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown supports an artist who is looking to leave London to realise her ambition of living life in the West Yorkshire countryside. Steve also visits the town of Todmorden and discovers a remarkable community project encouraging the planting and growing of food in public spaces for anyone to harvest and enjoy

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea and Steve Fletcher restore the star of an iconic exhibit at the Eden Project in Cornwall, hoping to put a new lease of life into the mechanical cat thats already run more than one and a half million times. Will Kirk repairs a Welsh love spoon, carved by a father passionate about keeping traditional craft alive

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which Michael, Bethany, Sam, Lou and David race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong and Ed Gamble

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: Comic Relief
programme.desc: Davina McCall, Joel Dommett, Catherine Tate as Nan, Nick Mohammed and Katherine Ryan host the annual fundraising extravaganza from Media City UK, Salford. Including a world exclusive of Traitors: The Movie: The Sequel, and Amandaland returns with a sketch full of famous Amandas. Dermot OLeary and Alison Hammond find a creative but very risky way to raise money for Comic Relief - robbing a bank. Radio 1 DJ Greg James finishes his mammoth challenge of riding a tandem bike 1,000km across the UK in just seven days, going from Weymouth to Edinburgh then making his way to Media City to join the fundraising in the studio. Four content creators take on one of the hardest songs of the century in the name of a good cause and there are films throughout highlighting the charitys work

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: The presenter is joined by comedian and award-winning podcaster Joanne McNally, who speaks to Claudia about her record-breaking stand-up tour, Pinotphile. Bafta-nominated comedian and actor Guz Khan speaks about his new Amazon Prime series Bait, which he stars in alongside Riz Ahmed. Former One Direction star and singer-songwriter Niall Horan joins Claudia to share how he went from global boy band success to solo chart-topping artist and talk about his new album, featuring his latest single Dinner Party

programme.title: RuPauls Drag Race UK vs the World
programme.desc: Michelle Visage, Graham Norton and Alan Carr join RuPaul on the judging panel as the final queens face off in the ultimate Lip-Sync Smackdown to find out who will be crowned Queen of the Mothertucking World. Plus, Michelle hosts a reunion, inviting the eliminated queens to return and reflect on the highs and lows of the series with the finalists

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps with a house hunt in the Bedfordshire borders, where a property with as much natural light as possible is top of the shopping list

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore needs to find a way to make a profit from a vintage set of trunks and upholsterer Ollie Clueit tries to nurse a dated chair back from the brink

programme.title: The Addams Family 2 (2021)
programme.desc: Lawyer Mr Mustela approaches Gomez and Morticia for a sample of their daughter Wednesdays DNA. The advocate claims the enfant terrible was switched in the maternity ward with another child, leaving Gomez devastated at the prospect of losing his little girl. Animated family comedy, with the voices of Oscar Isaac and Charlize Theron and Chloe Grace Moretz

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: The series concludes with a look at the work of people striving to protect Asias endangered wildlife, and what routes conservation could take in the future. In Japan, scientists take pregnant sharks, killed accidentally in fishing nets, and rescue their unborn young in an incubating system that simulates a womb. The programme also visits a village that used to be a hotbed of poaching and is now a centre for bird tourism

programme.title: BBC News

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh host the last semi-final. Four contenders must face five brutal events before going head to head in the legendary Eliminator for a place in next weeks final. Steel has a score to settle in Hang Tough, Viper charges into Suspension Bridge, and Fire enjoys an epic fight with a firefighter

programme.title: Michael McIntyres The Wheel
programme.desc: The comedian hosts the Saturday-night game show, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing a cash prize. Celebrities experts offering advice are Anita Rani on Women, Babatunde Aleshe on Eddie Murphy, Ekin-Su on Beauty, Gyles Brandreth on King Charles III, Josh Widdicombe on Train Stations, Nick Knowles on DIY and Stacey Dooley on Strictly Come Dancing

programme.title: The Weakest Link
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan presents a Gladiators special, posing general knowledge questions against the clock to Bionic, Sabre, Giant, Diamond, Fire and Athena, as well as referee Mark Clattenburg and commentator Guy Mowbray. Expect fierce competition as they battle to win £50,000 for their chosen charity

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Cam, Indie and Siobhan face the devastating consequences of their actions, while a bus crash produces a mystery when an elderly patients husband claims to have been travelling alone. Kim tries to conceal the fact that her vision is blurring while treating a patient, while Iain is struck by the relationship between a teenage girl and her father

programme.title: The Walsh Sisters
programme.sub-title: If You Say It Out Loud, Its Real
programme.desc: Hurt by Annas absence, Rachel becomes even more unsettled when Claire accidentally lets something slip. Maggie receives big news of her own and later crosses paths with Luke, who is still shaken by his last encounter with Rachel. At the flat, Anna is caught off guard by an unexpected visit from Janie, who arrives with startling news

programme.title: BBC News

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Everton v Chelsea at Hill Dickinson Stadium, and Leeds United v Brentford at Elland Road. A 2-0 victory for the Blues in Decembers reverse fixture brought an end to a four-game winless run Chelsea were embroiled in at the time, while Dominic Calvert-Lewin rescued a point for the Whites in a 1-1 draw when they travelled to Brentford earlier in the campaign. Plus, action from Brighton and Hove Albion v Liverpool, Fulham v Burnley, and Bournemouth v Manchester United

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy receives news of the untimely death of boxing legend Ricky Hatton, which reminds him to live life to its fullest. He then sets out on the most gruelling test of endurance possible - climbing the three highest mountains in England, Scotland and Wales in just 24 hours. At Halloween, Tommy and John go pumpkin picking with Bambi, things are back on track with Molly, and the house renovation is almost completed

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Weird Weekends
programme.desc: The agnostic presenter goes in search of salvation in Americas Bible Belt, where Christian TV channel host Marcus Lamb invites him to be born again live on air as a highlight of his show

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Weird Weekends
programme.desc: The intrepid reporter delves into Americas porn industry, meeting veteran actress Sharon Mitchell, who featured in the infamous 1970s blue movie Debbie Does Dallas. Later, he poses nude for a talent agent and gets some first-hand advice from an adult-movie star

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise
programme.desc: Zara McDermott experiences Ko Phangans famous Full Moon Party, but beneath the cheap cocktails, music and fire shows, theres danger lurking as the medical team treat a possible drink-spiking victim. She then travels to Phuket and experiences a taste of Thailands luxury lifestyle. But she also learns how dream holidays can quickly go wrong, hearing the case of a British holiday-maker who died on the island - and whose family is still searching for answers

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse (2018)
programme.desc: After being bitten by a radioactive arachnid, student Miles Morales develops incredible abilities, and becomes a version of Spider-Man. When a super collider device opens a gateway to other dimensions, Miles crosses paths with five counterparts to stop a threat for all realities. Oscar-winning animated adventure, with the voices of Shameik Moore and Nicolas Cage

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: A man afflicted with chronic bad luck tries to reconnect with his dad after setting fire to the family home. Comedy short, starring Dylan Blore and Mark Bonnar

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Weird Weekends
programme.desc: The sceptical reporter travels to Americas South West to find out why this dustbowl, with little to recommend it to its sparse human population, should be so irresistible to aliens, who have allegedly been visiting for more than 50 years. Along the way he meets a self-styled warrior monk who has declared war on evil extraterrestrials

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis interviews some of the biggest names in UK entertainment and is granted exclusive access into their lives at home and at work, beginning with platinum-selling rapper Stormzy. After two years out of the spotlight, Stormzy welcomes Louis into his home in south-west London to sit down for a frank and revealing conversation that covers many aspects of his life; from his childhood to the breakdown of a high-profile relationship, and the pressures that come with being one of the countrys best-loved musicians and role models

programme.title: Nerve (2016)
programme.desc: A teenager finds herself immersed in an online game of truth or dare, where her every move starts to be manipulated by an anonymous community of watchers. Soon, the challenges she and her fellow players are set grow more dangerous, until lives are genuinely at stake. Thriller, starring Emma Roberts and Dave Franco

programme.title: EastEnders Investigates: The Manosphere
programme.desc: An investigation into the issues of misogyny and toxic masculinity, to coincide with the current EastEnders storyline centred on teenager Joel Marshall, played by Max Murray

programme.title: Men of the Manosphere
programme.desc: James Blake enters the virtual world of manhood, often referred to as the manosphere, in search of status, wealth and relationships. He meets three young men who have turned to male social media influencers for guidance and instruction as they seek to better themselves and achieve success in different areas of their lives, both on and offline

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Weird Weekends
programme.desc: The sceptical reporter travels to Americas South West to find out why this dustbowl, with little to recommend it to its sparse human population, should be so irresistible to aliens, who have allegedly been visiting for more than 50 years. Along the way he meets a self-styled warrior monk who has declared war on evil extraterrestrials

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis interviews some of the biggest names in UK entertainment and is granted exclusive access into their lives at home and at work, beginning with platinum-selling rapper Stormzy. After two years out of the spotlight, Stormzy welcomes Louis into his home in south-west London to sit down for a frank and revealing conversation that covers many aspects of his life; from his childhood to the breakdown of a high-profile relationship, and the pressures that come with being one of the countrys best-loved musicians and role models

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy receives news of the untimely death of boxing legend Ricky Hatton, which reminds him to live life to its fullest. He then sets out on the most gruelling test of endurance possible - climbing the three highest mountains in England, Scotland and Wales in just 24 hours. At Halloween, Tommy and John go pumpkin picking with Bambi, things are back on track with Molly, and the house renovation is almost completed

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor takes Clara on her first trip into outer space, travelling to the inhabited rings surrounding the planet Akhaten. Clara befriends a young girl who has been selected to take part in a sacred ritual, while an ancient and powerful being awakens from its slumber, to feast on the lives of the planets inhabitants

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor and Clara land on a damaged Russian submarine in 1983 as it sinks into the ocean depths. To make matters worse, an alien warrior from Mars is loose on board, having escaped from a block of Arctic ice. With tempers flaring and a cargo of nuclear weapons, the whole of humanity at stake. David Warner, Liam Cunningham and Tobias Menzies guest star in an episode featuring the return of classic foes the Ice Warriors

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: The spooks thoughtless behaviour sends Alison into meltdown, prompting them to come up with a creative way to get back into her good books and show her how truly sorry they are. Mary however, believing she has done nothing wrong, refuses to take any responsibility, not after the fiery fate that befell her the last time she took the blame for something she didnt do. Alison and Mike have a last-minute cancellation for the guesthouse and decide that a relaxing day off is exactly what they need. Comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A shocking revelation throws the Ghosts into disarray. Kitty looks to Pat for moral support, and while he is more than happy to oblige, he finds himself in deep water trying to offer her words of comfort. Another bombshell drops when Mike realises there have been some crossed wires over an event booking and he and Alison are forced into hosting an impromptu - and ill-prepared - childrens birthday bash in order not to let two little girls down. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: A secretive phone call from a solicitor to Mrs Brown leaves her children suspecting one of them may be adopted, while Father Quinn returns from rehab and asks Agnes to help out at his annual garden party - although as she proceeds to drive him crazy with a smartphone, his recovery may be short-lived. Meanwhile, Cathy receives a blast from the past, Rory and Dino have a bust-up, and Mark and Betty deliver bad news - they are leaving for Australia right away. Domestic comedy, starring Brendan OCarroll

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: The Browns are excited as preparations for Rory and Dinos big day are in full swing - although Agness mood is dampened when she meets flamboyant celebrity wedding planner La La Doggy. The foul-mouthed matriarch also clashes with Father Damien when he raises religious concerns about the ceremony - and to top it all off, she gets more than she bargained for when she invites Cathys boyfriends parents over for the evening. Hit comedy, starring Brendan OCarroll, with Jennifer Gibney, Rory Cowan and Conor Moloney

programme.title: Alan Partridge: Alpha Papa (2013)
programme.desc: The hapless local radio DJ ends up in the centre of a siege when colleague Pat Farrell gets fired by the stations new owners, only to respond by returning to the studio with a shotgun and taking hostages. The police enlist Alans aid as a negotiator, giving him the chance to revive his own flagging public profile. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Colm Meaney and Felicity Montagu

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Gaz tries to ease his guilt by doing something special for Donna, and Janet throws a party in honour of Louises graduation. Meanwhile, Jonny and Kate are more concerned with finding somewhere private. Will Mellor and Natalie Casey star

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janets new boss makes her life a misery, while Gaz resolves to split the lovely Kate from Jonny, who in turn receives an ultimatum to dump his friends or face a return to being single. Comedy, starring Sheridan Smith and Will Mellor. First broadacst in 2004

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Donnas tutor invites her to have a little extracurricular fun, but she is shocked when it takes place on his sofa. Gaz experiences problems in the bedroom and Janet urges a reluctant Jonny to sample her latest pasty recipe

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Donna enjoys some filthy brunching with her tutor, while Louise gets Jonny into trouble with the police. Gaz solves his bedroom problem and Janet becomes a vociferous advocate of adventurous sex

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: Comedy about five talented black teenagers at a prestigious boarding school. As the boarders gear up for their final term, a shocking act of vandalism throws everything into chaos. With the school on lockdown, Toby has to find a way to break out and see Abby. Leah is under suspicion for the school destruction, and Femi is distracted by a mystery girl. Jaheim wrestles with a dilemma that will change everything

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: The students are teamed up with career mentors, but things quickly go wrong. Leah is fuming with Felix about the sabotage, and Femis dreams are crushed. Omar is in denial, and Toby is distracted by Abbys dangerous games. Violence erupts on the hockey pitch, and the students bright futures feel further away than ever

programme.title: EastEnders Investigates: The Manosphere
programme.desc: An investigation into the issues of misogyny and toxic masculinity, to coincide with the current EastEnders storyline centred on teenager Joel Marshall, played by Max Murray

programme.desc: Tim Renkow welcomes comedians Janine Harouni and Stuart Goldsmith to perform their stand-up routines on Londons Hammersmith Apollo stage

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining three chefs from Wales serve their mains and desserts to judge Tommy Banks. Including guinea fowl in tribute to Welsh-Zambian director Rungano Nyoni and a dish inspired by Snow White that uses frozen snow

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team investigate the murder of a local newspapers notorious agony aunt, poisoned the morning after her retirement party while reading her final advice column. Mervin resists the counselling sessions the Commissioner has imposed after the case in Antigua and gets a rude awakening from an unexpected figure

programme.title: The Walsh Sisters
programme.sub-title: This Wasnt the Plan. But Its Where We Are
programme.desc: Anna feels weighed down by emotions she cant release, while Rachel is unable to sleep and desperate to quiet her racing thoughts. Claire takes Kate to the doctor, learning she needs glasses, before scrambling to prepare a costume for a last-minute dress-up day. Maggie confides that she and Garv are trying for a baby, while Luke decides to visit Rachel in rehab

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: A behind the scenes look at the making of the programme, the survival expert shares the ideas and planning involved in the shows creation and how the tasks were filmed, whatever the weather. With input from the mediators, therapist and psychologist who guided those involved through their wild experience

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Experts at the barn tackle a monster-hunting camera lost in the depths of Loch Ness for 55 years. In Glasgow, Dom Chinea enlists a local stained-glass restorer to help put a shattered treasure back together, while sparks fly as Will Kirk learns how to make a bespoke bike frame in the Highlands

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh host the first of this years semi-finals. Over five gruelling events and the energy-draining Eliminator, only the fastest and strongest will make it to the grand final. The referees are on point in Collision, unbeaten Comet returns to Hang Tough, and Cyclone gets bored on top of Everest

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: The journalist in conversation with people who influence our political thinking about what has shaped theirs

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.sub-title: 2026 World Indoor Championships: Day One Morning Session
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on day one of the event, held this year at Kujawsko-Pomorska Arena Torun in Poland. The mens heptathlon gets under way with the 60m, long jump and shot put, and the first medals of the championship are awarded in the womens high jump, which saw Australians Nicola Olyslager and Eleanor Patterson finish first and second respectively last year

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: The current set of players faces its final day competing for the chance to win £10,000 before 24 new faces arrive. Game show, hosted by Rick Edwards

programme.title: The Tournament
programme.desc: Eight players do battle in the knockout quiz. Only one will be left standing at the end, hoping to reach the Golden Run and double their money. Hosted by Alex Scott

programme.title: Chess Masters: The Endgame
programme.desc: In the second heat, six new players face a gruelling game of speed chess and a puzzle called The Raging Rook, which allows the players to showcase their mastery of one of the most powerful pieces. Presented by Sue Perkins, with analysis from David Howell and Anthony Mathurin

programme.title: Clive Myries Italian Road Trip
programme.desc: The newsreader explores the coastline around Mount Vesuvius, famous for the ruins of Pompeii. He unearths why volcanic soil makes for the tastiest fruit and vegetables, learns to make Italian marmalade, uncovers the surprising royal tale behind Italys first trainline and tries his hand at carving jewellery from coral found deep beneath the sea

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The great migration leads to a series of confrontations for the families on the great savannah. Tragedy strikes for lioness Kali, forcing her to make a difficult decision. Bakari challenges the jealous leader of the baboon troop, leading to a violent battle, and elephant mother Nalla has to decide the future of her badly behaved teenager Tembo. Shani the zebra mother has no choice but to cross the river, fully aware a crocodile is waiting - and if he does not get her, the currents might well sweep her away

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Elizabeth Talbot and Paul Laidlaw hunt for antiques in the Bowes Museum at Barnard Castle in Co Durham, with finds including a silver tea set and a Russian drinking cup. Plus, Paul Martin looks at the museums lace collection

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.sub-title: 2026 World Indoor Championships: Day One Evening Session
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day one of the event, held this year at Kujawsko-Pomorska Arena Torun in Poland. The mens 60m final takes centre stage, and Jeremiah Azu of Great Britain was the victorious athlete in that event last year in Nanjing, China, finishing just one hundredth of a second clear of Australias Lachlan Kennedy. Medals are also awarded in the womens shot put and mens triple jump

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob and Paul return to the Peak District, this time with Ted, where they filmed their first episode of Gone Fishing six years ago. They start by fishing in the Wye at Haddon Hall, which was built in the 11th century and has been visited by countless kings and queens, with Paul on a quest to catch a rainbow trout. They then head off to the Derwent, where they compare their and Teds health to that of the UKs rivers

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Spring is in full stride at Longmeadow, and Monty Don gets straight to work, bringing new borders to life, reviving winter-worn houseplants and priming his pelargoniums for a standout summer. Joe Swift enjoys access to the private garden of renowned designer Mary Keen, and at RHS Garden Rosemoor, Carol Klein reveals how fruit and veg can deliver both flavour and flair - no matter the size of your plot

programme.title: Comic Relief Does The Weakest Link
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a special episode The Weakest Link. Six comedians take to the floor in an attempt to win bragging rights for themselves and prize money for Comic Relief

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events with Matt Chorley

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: In Camera (2023)
programme.desc: Aden is a young actor with ambition but stuck in a cycle of nightmarish auditions. When the equally ambitious Conrad, a brash entrepreneur joins him and junior doctor Bo in a flat share, Aden takes it upon himself to find a new part to play. Drama, starring Nabhaan Rizwan and Amir El-Masry

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: BBC Medical Editor Fergus Walsh reports on the future of assisted dying in the UK, travelling to Western Australia, where voluntary assisted dying has been available since 2021 to meet one of the doctors who pioneered it, and to a patient preparing for an assisted death. He also talks to leading supporters and opponents in the UK, and visits a hospice to talk to patients at the end of their lives

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from Wales must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. They need to impress Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee, Phil Wang and guest judge Sally El Hosaini, the Bafta-nominated director of acclaimed film The Swimmers. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: Period drama based on Janice Hadlows novel, telling the story of the overlooked middle sister in Pride and Prejudice, both during the events of Jane Austens classic novel and beyond. The Meryton assembly brings Marys first taste of romance, but also leaves her forced to decide between trying to please her family and forging a path of her own. Starring Ella Bruccoleri and Richard E Grant

programme.title: The Other Bennet Sister
programme.desc: After disappointment at the ball, Mary sets aside hopes of finding a match and devotes herself to more serious pursuits. But Marys resolve is tested when Mr Bennets distant cousin, Mr Collins, visits Longbourn with a surprising proposal. Mary contemplates a decision which could save her family from ruin, but is she really willing to suppress her true feelings to secure her familys future?

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.sub-title: Battling Islamic State Group in Somalia
programme.desc: The team follows Puntland forces on the front line in far-eastern Somalia, examining how the Islamic State group has expanded its influence across Africa. Plus, Kalkidan Yibeltal has rare access to Jonglei state in South Sudan, where renewed conflict threatens a fragile peace accord

programme.desc: A salesman visits convinces the Piris to buy extra flippers. The penguins discover that their purchases are duds but find a clever new use for them

programme.sub-title: Sabre, Legend, Cyclone, Nitro v the Hotel!
programme.desc: Nitro, Cyclone, Legend and Sabre compete at a hotel check-in desk as they dare each other with outrageous instructions for the chance to win or tap out and risk losing

programme.title: Blue Peter

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Current affairs reports aimed at a younger audience

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.sub-title: 2026 World Indoor Championships: Day Two Morning Session
programme.desc: Coverage of the morning session on day two at Kujawsko-Pomorska Arena Torun in Poland. The mens heptathlon continues with the 60m hurdles and pole vault, while the heats of the mens 60m hurdles also take place, before the session concludes with the mens and womens 800m semi-finals. The other notable event is the final of the mixed 4x400m relay, which is making its debut at this championship

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Adam Partridge and Christina Trevanion value objects at Hereford Cathedral, where one item goes over the £1,000 mark at auction. Paul Martin explores his craft-making skills as he tries to make a traditional wooden stool

programme.title: The Third Man (1949)
programme.desc: American writer Holly Martins goes to post-Second World War Vienna at the invitation of his old friend Harry Lime, only to be told that Lime has been killed by a car while crossing the street. However, the author finds the circumstances surrounding his death fail to add up and goes in search of the truth - but is unprepared for the ugly secrets he uncovers. Carol Reeds thriller, written by Graham Greene, starring Joseph Cotten, Orson Welles, Alida Valli and Trevor Howard

programme.title: Super League Live
programme.desc: Warrington Wolves v Castleford Tigers (Kick-off 3.00pm). Coverage of the top-flight match from Halliwell Jones Stadium. The Wolves scored six tries in a 34-24 victory when the teams met here last season, with Josh and Jake Thewlis, Rodrick Tai, and Arron Lindop all crossing the line in the first half, and Ben Currie and Stefan Ratchford doing likewise in the second, with the latter also making his 350th appearance for Warrington

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.sub-title: 2026 World Indoor Championships: Day Two Evening Session
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day two at Kujawsko-Pomorska Arena Torun in Poland. The mens heptathlon concludes with the 1,000m, which last year saw Norways Sander Skotheim claim the gold medal with a narrow victory over Johannes Erm, despite the Estonians victory in this event, as he had to settle for silver. Elsewhere, gold medals will also be awarded in eight disciplines, including the mens and womens 400m and 3,000m, the mens 60m hurdles, and womens 60m

programme.title: Comic Reliefs Hits and Hidden Gems at the BBC
programme.desc: Scott Mills looks at the greatest musical moments from the long-running charity, including chart-topping performances by Adele, One Direction and the Spice Girls, as well as rarely seen highlights from Sam Smith and John Legend, Justin Bieber, Marti Pellow, McFly - and Helen Mirren and Robbie Coltrane

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A celebration of the biggest songs from 1987. Performers include Rick Astley, Whitney Houston, TPau, Mel and Kim, Curiosity Killed the Cat, Eric B and Rakim, Pet Shop Boys, Alison Moyet and Boy George

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A celebration of the best of the years programmes, including performances by Yazz, SExpress, Bomb the Bass, Kylie Minogue, Jason Donovan, Bros, Tiffany, Womack and Womack, Enya, Maxi Priest and Everything But the Girl. Plus, the Proclaimers, Neneh Cherry, Tanita Tikaram, Eurythmics, Ofra Haza and Fairground Attraction

programme.title: Aftersun (2022)
programme.desc: Sophie reflects on the shared joy and private melancholy of a holiday she took with her father 20 years earlier. Memories real and imagined fill the gaps between as she tries to reconcile the father she knew with the man she didnt. Drama, starring Frankie Corio and Paul Mescal

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: More video evidence of the attack emerges. Careys assertion that an insidious conspiracy is unfolding at the heart of the establishment gains little traction, so she takes matters into her own hands and seeks an unlikely ally in Gemma Garland. Meanwhile, Noah Pierson finds his first day at work more surprising than he ever expected

programme.title: Small Prophets
programme.desc: The bottled prophets are looking unwell and Michael still hasnt asked his questions. Brian is causing havoc at the nursing home and warns Michael and Kacey that time is of the essence, but on their return, all hell has broken out in Marvin Gardens., with the cul-de-sac crammed full of vehicles, strangers and irate neighbours

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Curse of Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: The team investigates a large, invisible bubble anomaly using drones, specifically within the Triangle area of the ranch. The analysis involves aerial experiments and the use of LiDAR scanning to map the phenomenon

programme.title: Encounter: UFO
programme.desc: The intimidating experience of an aircraft that encountered an object that failed to appear on radar. Who are they and what do they want?

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The ancient mysteries that exist on the islands of Malta, which could be connected to alien visitation. These are enormous, megalithic structures and a vast underground complex containing the remains of what some believe to be otherworldly beings

programme.title: Mountain Men
programme.desc: In Montana, long-time trapper Tom Oar struggles with heart problems, but is reluctant to give up his Mountain Man way of life

programme.title: Swamp People: Serpent Invasion
programme.desc: Troy Landry and Pickle Wheat mix it up with some locals. Tes puts her skills to the test hunting without her partner, Bill. Zak and Bruce return to Viper Alley, while Dusty hunts for a legend

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Ashley questions whether the pawn store has a thief in its midst when two teens come in with news of a scam

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and antiques dealers Tim Hogarth, Cheryl and David Hakeney, and Alison Chapman visit Morecambe, Lancashire, where a blunderbuss rumoured to be linked to Captain Kidd washes up in the den, along with an item from a Chinese shipwreck and an impressive inkwell

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.sub-title: I Came, I Saw, I Pawned
programme.desc: A seller strolls in with a collection of action figures signed by the cast of The Walking Dead. Corey is too cool when a Rock n Roll High School poster comes into the shop. Chum tries to catch an unusual Pokemon card

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.sub-title: Cotswold Oil Engine and Preservation Society Sale
programme.desc: Henry Cole and his partners Allen Millyard and Guy Willison are back on the road trawling Britains sheds and auction houses for rusty relics to restore and sell on for a tidy profit - this time they head down to Dorset to meet Tim, the owner of a delectable collection of trinkets. After coming away with some choice items, they return to the Ledbury auctions for yet more items before heading down the road to the Cotswold Oil Engine and Preservation Societys famous bring and buy sale

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: The latest round of auctions prompts a career-changing bid from Ivy, while Jarrod and Brandis Long Beach store is mired in gossip

programme.title: Storage Hunters

programme.title: Storage Hunters

programme.desc: A farmer discovers a cow mysteriously mutilated, a man is repeatedly abducted from his bed, and an astronomers telescope intercepts a mysterious message from outer space

programme.title: Weird or What? With William Shatner
programme.desc: Decoding ancient Mayan texts, a physicist predicts a cataclysm will destroy the world. Plus, a Nasa scientist makes an incredible alien discovery, and one of the worlds greatest minds makes a startling prediction about a sinister robot takeover

programme.title: Weird or What? With William Shatner
programme.desc: A woman who claims to have encountered shadow people and a man who claims to have used teleportation, travelling from New Jersey to New Mexico in the blink of an eye

programme.title: Weird or What? With William Shatner
programme.desc: Lights above the Indian Ocean send a nation into panic, and on Lake Michigan a yacht vanishes. Plus, a small Newfoundland community is devastated by a mysterious explosion

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: Veteran actor William Shatner explores the Earth and beyond, in search of the universes most fascinating, strange and inexplicable mysteries. This opening episode documents unidentified objects witnessed by military pilots, extraterrestrial probes flying through the solar system and a potential alien home world that may be hidden in the darkness of space

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: Veteran actor William Shatner examines the possibility of contact with otherworldly intelligent life, and how man should react to and handle such a situation. Plus, the practicality of man travelling beyond the moon

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: William Shatner addresses Earth itself, which remains in large part a mystery to the eight billion people who live on it. He analyses how Earth came to exist, and the secrets of its survival and contemplates how long humans have left to call it home

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: Humanity has worshipped the Sun since the beginning of time, and yet even with modern technology, so little is known about it. William Shatner considers whether unlocking the mysteries of the Sun can be the difference between life and death for human civilization

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.desc: As the next era of space exploration begins it must be analysed what humans will find when reaching other planets, as well as the dangers faced when trying to conquer new frontiers. Presented by William Shatner

programme.title: The UnXplained: Mysteries of the Universe
programme.sub-title: The Life and Death of the Sun
programme.desc: Humanity has worshipped the Sun since the beginning of time, and yet even with the help of modern technology there is much that is still not known about it. Veteran actor William Shatner explores its creation and finite status. He outlines what is likely to happen as it nears its life-end

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: How, at a mysterious site in the Gobi Desert, experts discovered a monster graveyard - a groundbreaking find containing treasures of alien beasts

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Documentary series exploring anomalies appearing on satellite images to try and get a better understanding of unexplained phenomena

programme.title: What on Earth?
programme.desc: Documentary series exploring anomalies appearing on satellite images to try and get a better understanding of unexplained phenomena

programme.title: Historys Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: Actor Laurence Fishburne examines the possible causes of mysterious vanishings in the Bermuda Triangle, a 500,000 square-mile area of the Atlantic where ships, planes and unsuspecting travellers have been lost without a trace

programme.title: Historys Greatest Mysteries
programme.sub-title: The Hunt for Stolen Nazi Treasure
programme.desc: During the Second World War, the Nazis stole vast amounts of art, gold, silver and currency, and stashed it across Europe. While some of the plunder has been recovered, much more is still missing nearly 80 years later

programme.title: Historys Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: The story of Noahs ark, the biblical tale in which God sends a flood to wipe out nearly every living thing on Earth, except for Noah, his family and the animals he saves in his fabled ark. This episode details the search for the ark

programme.title: Holy Marvels with Dennis Quaid
programme.desc: For millennia, plagues, curses, droughts and the violent destruction of cities have been blamed on the wrath of angry gods. The presenter explores whether these divine punishments were mere myth, or there is any proof they are based on real events

programme.title: Ivan the Terrible
programme.desc: For four centuries the name Ivan the Terrible has conjured up the image of a vicious, cruel despot. This documentary investigates whether this is a purely Western view. In his own land, Russias first tsar is remembered as a statesman who held the country together through difficult times - and perhaps was not so terrible after all

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: Ancient practices such as prayer, blood sports, voodoo and exorcism are often thought to be relics of bygone superstition, though there is evidence they have an impact on the physical world. Is it possible that the mind and body can use the power of ritual to tap into hidden forces that lie just beyond the grasp of conventional explanation?

programme.title: The UnXplained with William Shatner
programme.desc: Perhaps the greatest unexplained mystery of all is what happens after death. Clues to the answer may lie in unusual cases of near-death experience, reincarnation, and how memories can be passed from organ donors to transplant recipients. The veteran actor considers if these phenomena are figments of mans collective imagination or whether they could be real

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: A look at Americas network of national parks, vast, dense expanses of virtually unexplored wilderness. This programme visits the Grand Canyon region, where a strange series of events leads researchers to speculate whether its power comes from forces that cannot be explained

programme.title: National Park Mysteries
programme.desc: A plane crash in the Everglades National Park leads to strange hauntings - and some believe the park may exert a dark, transformative power

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: Reported accounts of alien encounters, hearing from people who believe that beings from other worlds have the means to control humanity

programme.title: Alien Files: Unsealed
