programme.title: Miss Scarlet and the Duke
programme.desc: When Eliza Scarlets father dies, he leaves her penniless in a time where marriage is the only option for financial security. However, Eliza has an ace up her bonnet - his detective agency business. However, to operate in this mans world the headstrong beneficiary needs a partner. Crime drama set in Victorian London, starring Kate Phillips and Stuart Martin

programme.title: Miss Scarlet and the Duke
programme.desc: Eliza takes a seemingly impossible case: proving the innocence of a man found at a murder scene with a bloodied knife in his hand, who has already pleaded guilty

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: When the beaten body of wealthy German businessman Gunter Schmeikel is discovered in the harbour, Gently and Bacchus are forced to consider whether a barmans anti-German sentiments are a motive for murder. However, the case takes a sinister turn when a key witness to Schmeikels death is revealed to be a killer trained by Army Special Forces. Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby star, with Tim Healy

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Rivalries escalate to murderous heights at the Kembleston Olimpicks. A Hambleston competitor is poisoned during the spinach-eating contest, but it soon transpires that someone else was the intended target. Mrs Devine is shocked when her son Eddie returns to Kembleford. She is determined to keep her new relationship with Chief Inspector Sullivan a secret from everyone, although Father Brown and Brenda know exactly whats going on. Mystery drama, starring Mark Williams, with Claudie Blakley, Ruby-May Martinwood and Barney Wilkinson

programme.desc: The ecclesiastical sleuth investigates when his old friend Sister Boniface is implicated in the murder of a renowned artist. Mystery drama, starring Mark Williams, with Claudie Blakley and Lorna Watson

It looks like a simple case of suicide, but with Jack Mooney no longer around to confirm Commissioner Selwyns suspicions, DI Neville Parker is temporarily flown over from Manchester to close the case. However, he soon realises that island life is not for him, so when evidence of suspicious activity turns up, he is dismayed to realise he will have to extend his stay and investigate the case as murder. Ralf Little joins the cast, with guest appearances by Steve Pemberton and Samantha Bond

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Roman Catholic priest spies trouble when Lady Felicia returns to Kembleford from Africa, having split from her lover Benedict Northam. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Another mystery for Ardal OHanlon as Irish detective Jack Mooney working on the Caribbean island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. And so it appears this week as someone from JPs past is caught red-handed at a murder scene, raking up old memories for the young officer. But can the DI unravel the complex mystery to get at the truth? Tobi Bakare and Danny John-Jules co-star, with guest appearances from Ralph Brown and Adjoa Andoh

programme.title: My Life Is Murder
programme.desc: As Alexas birthday approaches, Kieran gives her an early present - the case of a plastic surgeon obsessed with getting her own cosmetic procedures who died after self-administering an anti-wrinkle drug she had developed. Detective drama, starring Lucy Lawless and Bernard Curry

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games

programme.desc: A lovelorn Mervyn is driven to distraction by a woman working at the local pizza place. When he reveals that he lacks the courage to profess his feelings for her, Alvin and Entwistle decide that is it up to them to bring the would-be couple together. Comedy, starring Philip Fox

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg and Truly reminisce about Compo, whose amorous intentions towards his neighbour Nora Batty - along with his accident-prone antics - were a constant source of amusement in the village

programme.desc: Flambeau is incarcerated under suspicion of murder, but he denies killing his cohort Flynn and asks Father Brown to hear his confession if he is convicted

However, when she asks the driver to open the door, things turn nasty and she is forced to radio for help. Catherine cant shake the feeling that Tommy is near and returns to the deserted house. Against police protocol she breaks in - and instantly realises something sinister has happened. Sarah Lancashire stars in the dark drama written by Sally Wainwright

programme.desc: A police informant resurfaces after 17 years on the run, and claims to have details implicating a crook-turned-celebrity in an unsolved armed robbery. programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Eric manages to land Lovejoy in hospital with a broken leg, so the incapacitated dealer tasks Lady Jane and Tinker with selling a terracotta Chinese pig to a Chinese banker on his behalf. programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: An antique Berber rug spells intrigue for the roguish dealer, leading him into a world of unrequited love and umbrella stands. Meanwhile, Lady Jane splits from her husband after refusing to accompany him to Hong Kong for his new job. Virginia McKenna guest stars

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The Rev Harry Nettles bets the antiques dealer that a field next to a church contains a Roman mosaic. Drama, guest starring David Kossoff, with Ian McShane and Dudley Sutton

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Flambeau is incarcerated under suspicion of murder, but he denies killing his cohort Flynn and asks Father Brown to hear his confession if he is convicted

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The gun used to kill a boxer is discovered at the scene of an armed robbery 15 years later, prompting the team to reinvestigate the case. Twenty-one-year-old Eddie Mayfair was shot dead a week after winning an important bout, which left his opponent seriously injured and unable to carry on his career. The detectives initially suspect revenge as a motive, but the case is complicated when they uncover the rivalries and money-making aspects of the sport. Guest starring Maggie ONeill (Shameless) and Chloe Howman (Casualty)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Retired DI Frank Patterson presents the team with information regarding the getaway car used in a 30-year-old safety deposit-box robbery. They discover the former detective not only wants to convict the suspected thief of the heist and murder of his three colleagues, but also prove that a high-ranking officer was involved in the plot - jeopardising the future of Sandras Ucos unit. Phil Daniels guest stars

programme.title: Jonathan Creek
programme.desc: A widow steeped in classical mythology is alarmed to discover her nightmares predict impending murders in vivid detail, until Jonathan becomes the killers nemesis and finds a more rational explanation closer to home. Drama, guest starring Rebecca Front, with Alan Davies and Caroline Quentin

programme.title: The Marlow Murder Club
programme.sub-title: The Queen of Poisons: Part One
programme.desc: Adaptation of Robert Thorogoods novel about a retired archaeologist who investigates murder. When the Mayor dies in the middle of a meeting, Judith, Becks and Suzie uncover lies, scandal and blackmail. Guest starring Peter Davison

programme.sub-title: The Queen of Poisons: Part Two

programme.desc: Mike and Beth take a long road trip to obtain divorce papers from his ex-wife, and Kristin and Daniel contend with the mysterious death of a man in a hotel room. Detective drama, starring Neill Rea

programme.title: Whitechapel
programme.desc: Part two of two. With the body count rising, Miles and Chandler clash over the direction of the investigation, and struggle to put aside their differences in the face of their toughest adversary to date. Counsellor Morgan Lamb is of particular interest to the team, having already survived the killers wrath, and Chandler has a vested interest in keeping her alive. Starring Rupert Penry-Jones, Phil Davis and Lydia Leonard

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A horse trainer with money worries is found hanged, and suicide is suspected until tests reveal he was drugged shortly before his death. Lynley discovers the cove on the victims estate was used to land contraband, and connects the death to a smuggling racket, but Havers suspects the answer lies with the local riding community. Detective drama, starring Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Sister Reginalds incorrigible brother Alfie Lynch, a habitual thief, turns up at the nunnery seeking help. He is put up at Mrs Clamss guest house. At an art exhibition in the local gallery, egocentric artist Gerry Ardwell features himself as the main exhibit. The next morning, Ardwell is found dead, bludgeoned by a brass statue, while a valuable painting has been stolen. Alfie Lynch is missing and DI Gillespies main suspect. Sister Reginald pleads with Sister Boniface to find the real culprit, a task made harder when the painting is found in Lynchs room and other suspects have alibis

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: Jim tries to censor part of a publication by the former prime minister that portrays him in a bad light. Political comedy, starring Paul Eddington, Nigel Hawthorne and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: Jim tries to turn the tables on French officials over the Channel Tunnel borders. Political comedy, starring Paul Eddington, Nigel Hawthorne and Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: Jim gets wind of a major financial scandal in the City that is bound to have damaging repercussions for the government. With the party conference looming, he decides its time for him to take swift and firm action - but Sir Humphrey has other ideas. Political comedy first broadcast in 1987, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: Jim feels local government isnt really representative, so is eager to hear more about proposals to reform the system. Sir Humphrey fears this would lead to the civil service losing its power and turns to an unlikely ally to help persuade the prime minister to think again. Political comedy first broadcast in 1988, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: Harry Enfields Television Programme
programme.desc: Classic sketch show featuring the many guises of Harry Enfield, Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke, including Smashie and Nicey, Kevin the Teenager, and The Slobs

programme.title: Im Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Alan insults Norfolks farmers with insensitive on-air remarks about the BSE crisis, and it is not long before they exact a particularly appropriate revenge. Steve Coogan stars, with guest appearances by Simon Pegg and Chris Morris

programme.title: Im Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Alans plan to watch all the James Bond films over the Bank Holiday weekend with Michael fall apart when his pal deserts him for the local American Truckfest - forcing him to decide whether to make a new friend or accompany Lynn to visit her mothers grave. Starring Steve Coogan and Felicity Montagu

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Gary finally prepares to give up his freedom but fears he has not slept with enough women, while Dorothy worries that gherkins in the pub will be the only highlight of her special day. Meanwhile, best man Tony has an ulterior motive for making sure the unhappy couple get through their ordeal safely. Comedy, starring Martin Clunes, Caroline Quentin, Neil Morrissey and Leslie Ash

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Joe Maplin ropes the reluctant Yellowcoats into entering the town carnival, and puts Spike in charge of designing their float. Jimmy Perry and David Crofts holiday camp comedy, starring Simon Cadell, Ruth Madoc and Jeffrey Holland

programme.title: Harry Enfields Television Programme
programme.desc: Classic sketch show featuring the many guises of Harry Enfield, Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke, including Smashie and Nicey, Kevin the Teenager, and The Slobs

programme.title: Dont Wait Up
programme.desc: Angela fears her holiday reconciliation with Toby may not survive their return to a daily domestic routine. Father-and-son comedy, starring Nigel Havers and Tony Britton

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Elgin spots a money-making opportunity when he finds out two psychic detectives are offering £5,000 for a photograph of a ghost. Meanwhile, Tyler prepares for his first gig with Puddle of Agony, but worries his credibility will be ruined when Boycie and Marlene decide to accompany him

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Dont Wait Up
programme.desc: Tom and Madeleine are married, while Toby realises Angelas lifestyle has changed since they have been separated. Nigel Havers and Tony Britton star

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr Grace offers the staff accommodation on the premises when their difficult journeys to work lead to demands for a travel allowance, but they soon find flat-sharing is not as easy as it seemed. Vintage comedy, starring John Inman and Mollie Sugden

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Marlene spots an opportunity to have her old possessions assessed by experts when the Antiques Roadshow arrives in Ludlow, and the farms staff all share the same idea. However, it turns out that Boycie, with help from Tyler, is the only one to discover a truly valuable item. Fiona Bruce guest stars

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr Spooner reveals unexpected musical abilities - and the rest of the staff, astonished to discover he has any talent at all, insist on cashing in on his chart success. Mike Berry, John Inman, Frank Thornton, Wendy Richard and Mollie Sugden star in the final episode of the long-running shopfloor comedy, which features a guest appearance by former Crimewatch presenter Nick Ross

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg comes off his bike after cycling downhill and sustains a fractured foot. Truly believes that the accident would never have happened if the vehicle had been fitted with studded tyres - and immediately sets about organising a practical demonstration of his theory

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Smiler reminisces about a woman who escaped his advances during the war without leaving her contact details, so Truly, Alvin and Billy volunteer to track her down - even though it means a foray into hostile territory. Long-running comedy series, starring Frank Thornton, Brian Murphy, Keith Clifford and Stephen Lewis

programme.title: Dads Army
programme.desc: Wilson allows the vicar and the verger to enlist in the Home Guard while Mainwaring is in hospital, but their over-enthusiastic approach to man-management brings the wrath of Warden Hodges down upon the platoon. Comedy, first shown in 1973, starring Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier and Bill Pertwee

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr Grace offers the staff accommodation on the premises when their difficult journeys to work lead to demands for a travel allowance, but they soon find flat-sharing is not as easy as it seemed. Vintage comedy, starring John Inman and Mollie Sugden

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth is waiting for a new three-piece suite to be delivered, boasting it is an exact replica of the one at Sandringham House - and goes to extraordinary lengths to ensure her neighbours see the royal warrant on the delivery van. But, as ever, her family contrives to take the gloss off her moment of glory. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: Dads Army
programme.desc: Captain Mainwaring and his platoon are ordered to clear the area for a major army exercise - but encounter a problem in the shape of a steam engine blocking the path. Classic comedy first broadcast in 1974, starring Arthur Lowe

programme.title: Dads Army
programme.desc: Wilson allows the vicar and the verger to enlist in the Home Guard while Mainwaring is in hospital, but their over-enthusiastic approach to man-management brings the wrath of Warden Hodges down upon the platoon. Comedy, first shown in 1973, starring Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier and Bill Pertwee

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Joe Maplin hatches a hare-brained scheme to stop a hospital being built next to the camp site, ordering staff to make a din from dawn till dusk while the council committee conduct an inspection visit. Jimmy Perry and David Crofts comedy, starring Ruth Madoc, Paul Shane and Su Pollard

programme.title: Are You Being Served?

Comedy, first shown in 1973, starring Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier and Bill Pertwee

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg comes off his bike after cycling downhill and sustains a fractured foot. Truly believes that the accident would never have happened if the vehicle had been fitted with studded tyres - and immediately sets about organising a practical demonstration of his theory

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Smiler reminisces about a woman who escaped his advances during the war without leaving her contact details, so Truly, Alvin and Billy volunteer to track her down - even though it means a foray into hostile territory. Long-running comedy series, starring Frank Thornton, Brian Murphy, Keith Clifford and Stephen Lewis

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth is waiting for a new three-piece suite to be delivered, boasting it is an exact replica of the one at Sandringham House - and goes to extraordinary lengths to ensure her neighbours see the royal warrant on the delivery van. But, as ever, her family contrives to take the gloss off her moment of glory. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr Grace offers the staff accommodation on the premises when their difficult journeys to work lead to demands for a travel allowance, but they soon find flat-sharing is not as easy as it seemed. Vintage comedy, starring John Inman and Mollie Sugden

programme.title: Dads Army
programme.desc: Captain Mainwaring and his platoon are ordered to clear the area for a major army exercise - but encounter a problem in the shape of a steam engine blocking the path. Classic comedy first broadcast in 1974, starring Arthur Lowe

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr Spooner reveals unexpected musical abilities - and the rest of the staff, astonished to discover he has any talent at all, insist on cashing in on his chart success. Mike Berry, John Inman, Frank Thornton, Wendy Richard and Mollie Sugden star in the final episode of the long-running shopfloor comedy, which features a guest appearance by former Crimewatch presenter Nick Ross

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: The veteran radio and TV presenter and the team host a special show dedicated to the memory of a much-cherished broadcaster. With viewers in shock, it falls to Alan to handle this delicate situation as only he can. But one mans loss is anothers opportunity. Comedy written by Rob and Neil Gibbons and star Steve Coogan, with Susannah Fielding as co-host Jennie Gresham

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: The Trotters meet a pregnant German woman who is in need of help, so eternal optimist Del-Boy becomes her saviour, aiming to earn himself some money into the bargain by making Marlene and Boycies dreams come true. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst, Buster Merryfield and John Challis

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: A weeping statue in the local church gives Del an idea for a money-making scheme to help a hospice under threat of closure. However, his efforts are thwarted by the weather. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: For once, Del-Boy is an innocent victim when a routine trip to the supermarket turns into a tense night as an inept robber holds the Trotters hostage, along with the manager and security guard. Comedy, starring David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Vas Blackwood

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Tempers flare between the Trotter brothers as they fall for the same woman - and Dels efforts to win the romance war leave his future hanging in the balance. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Rodney and Mickey Pearce are commissioned to make a community film, but Del-Boy soon gets in on the act, offering a role to anyone who wants one - provided they pay for the privilege

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.sub-title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: An old friend returns from Australia flushed with success and offers Del a share in his business empire Down Under. Although the prospect is tempting, it would mean leaving Nelson Mandela House and younger brother Rodney behind. David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst star

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del-Boy enters the import trade after striking a deal with Boycie to fetch a consignment of dodgy diamonds across from Holland, and ropes in Rodney and Uncle Albert to help. Their plans are jeopardised when old foe Chief Inspector Slater picks up the Trotters trail. Feature-length episode of the comedy, guest starring Jim Broadbent, with David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Buster Merryfield

programme.desc: Classic sketch show featuring the many guises of Harry Enfield, Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke, including Smashie and Nicey, Kevin the Teenager, and The Slobs

programme.desc: Angela fears her holiday reconciliation with Toby may not survive their return to a daily domestic routine. Father-and-son comedy, starring Nigel Havers and Tony Britton

programme.title: Deepfake Porn: Could You Be Next?
programme.desc: The rising trend of harmless images being turned into hardcore porn, footage that is often used against the people depicted with devastating consequences. The film features three women who have been impacted by this form of image-based sexual abuse, including a leading Florida State Senator, Lauren Book, the UK campaigner Kate Isaacs, and Dina, a woman who was deep-faked by a work colleague

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley meets Americans prepping for an uncertain future, all with different concerns - from climate change and natural disasters to civil unrest. They include a family of 12 in Colorados Rocky Mountains, who have isolated themselves for the past decade

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: Presenter Alex Jones starts her training as a fertility assistant at Kings Fertility, London, and is put straight to work caring for patients going through IVF treatment.

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: During her second week at Kings Fertility, Alex cares for patients going in and out of theatre and witnesses the emotional turmoil and resilience of the patients undergoing IVF

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: MasterChef Australia
programme.desc: That gleaming gold trophy is finally within reach as the Grand Finale of MasterChef 2023 is here

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien discover items including stained glass and a Victorian hay rack that are ready for upcycling to turn a profit for their owners

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets 47-year-old Stuart in Torquay, who has progressive multiple sclerosis. Despite being six foot seven, he sleeps in a chair in the living room. His primary carer is his 73-year-old mother, Grandma Lin, who is completely blind, and walks across town every day to see to her son and her granddaughter Laurens needs. Can the crack team of builders, plasterers and electricians help turn their lives around?

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Plymouth to meet the Morfey family, who sold their multi-level home and bought an old wrought-iron forge with the intention of renovating it for their two eldest sons, who have cerebral palsy. But when their daughter got leukaemia and moved miles away for treatment, supported by mum Tara, the family was torn apart. Now the house remains half-built, with no kitchen, hot water or living space. What the Morfeys need is a little help to bring the family back together

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Cameras catch up with the work of the pioneering veterinary practice, where some of the hardest-to-cure pets receive cutting-edge treatment. When one-year-old shepherd doodle Raven started limping at only a few months old, his local vet diagnosed double hip dysplasia. Now, owners Alan and Jane find themselves at Fitzpatricks to discuss possible total hip replacement for both back legs. Also receiving help are three-month-old kitten Fury, who has a suspected tibia injury sustained during boisterous play and two-year-old police dog Trigger who has degenerative lumbosacral stenosis, causing pain in his lower back

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: One-year-old golden retriever Bertie developed a dislocated hip joint and curvature of his spine and is in a lot of pain. Noel has to find an implant to treat Berties arthritis and prevent his hip from dislocating and work out a way to manage the effect of his spinal deformity over time. Trusty Tibetan terrier, nine-year-old Sunny recently received a total knee replacement using an implant system called a rotating hinge. However, before a second procedure is staged a flare up of the auto-immune inflammatory arthritis in Sunnys carpi casts a dark cloud over his future once again

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Hessle, near Hull in East Yorkshire, to transform the home of Darren Hudson, who was left paralysed from the waist down due to a spinal cord injury. After three months of hospital rehabilitation, Darren returned home to his family, but tragedy struck again eight days later when his wife Sarah died suddenly. Since that moment, Darren and his three sons have been living in emotional trauma, with the wheelchair-using dad now the main carer for the family

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles meets 47-year-old Stuart in Torquay, who has progressive multiple sclerosis. Despite being six foot seven, he sleeps in a chair in the living room. His primary carer is his 73-year-old mother, Grandma Lin, who is completely blind, and walks across town every day to see to her son and her granddaughter Laurens needs. Can the crack team of builders, plasterers and electricians help turn their lives around?

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The paramedics treat a young mum with severe stomach pains, take a breathless pensioner to hospital and attend an elderly lady with dementia whose nose will not stop bleeding

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: Featuring a stand-off involving a man wielding a samurai sword in South Yorkshire

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: HART staff must treat one of their own after a freak accident involving a 4x4 leaves an off-duty medic with serious injuries

programme.title: Women Behind Bars with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: Part one of two. The veteran broadcaster follows life inside two prisons holding some of Americas most notorious female criminals - Indiana Womens Prison and Rockville Correctional Facility in the same US state. Among the inmates he meets in the first edition are Sarah Pender, who went on the run while serving 110 years for double murder, before being captured four months later, and Martha Search, who is looking forward to getting to grips with modern technology when her 20-year sentence comes to an end

programme.title: Women Behind Bars with Trevor McDonald
programme.desc: Part two of two. The veteran broadcaster meets more inmates inside two of Indianas most dangerous womens prisons. Among them are Dawn Hopkins, who was sentenced to life for murdering her three-month-old son, and Sarah Cindy White, who was incarcerated as a teenager and will spend the rest of her life behind bars. The journalist also hears the remarkable story of Linda Darby who, after escaping from jail in 1972, spent the next 35 years on the run - before the law finally caught up with her

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: Alex is now one month into her training as a fertility assistant and she is finally able to work independently on the early pregnancy scan list

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: Alex spends her first shift in Andrology learning about how they analyse and store sperm. Later, she works with a patient whose only hope of having children is by using donor eggs

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: Too Hot to Handle - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of three. The evidence against undercover cop Stevie mounts up, suggesting she has thrown away her career for the love of a villain. Stuart finally catches up with the pair, and his suspicions are confirmed when she turns a gun on her colleague and makes her escape. With the violent Tameside Crew wreaking carnage across Sun Hill in pursuit of Towler, Stuart is suspended from duty for being too close to the case. Concludes tomorrow

programme.title: The Bill
programme.sub-title: Too Hot to Handle - Part Three
programme.desc: Part three of three. In a flashback sequence, Stevie reveals to Stuart how she discovered the drug deal was a set-up and why she disobeyed instructions in order to protect her boyfriend. Stuart is astonished that her erratic behaviour was designed to save his life and expose the real villain behind the Tameside Crew. As she makes plans to flee the country, the gang seems always to be two steps ahead of her - and Stevie still has to make the biggest decision of her life

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mels younger brother and his girlfriend come to stay on New Years Eve and she goes to work thinking the couple are happily celebrating in Trafalgar Square - until Sun Hill officers are called to investigate a stabbing in a club. With the troublesome youngster in the frame for the attack, apparently provoked by a fellow clubber who made advances to his companion, Mel sets out to persuade her colleagues he is innocent

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When a young mother is found badly beaten in an alleyway, Sun Hill CID is drawn into her tale of debt, blackmail and a complex love life. The victim was seen arguing with a former boyfriend in a nightclub the previous day, and DNA evidence recovered from under her fingernails leads to a man who she owed a huge amount of money to. Christopher Timothy and Duncan James guest star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. A couple return from a night away to discover their baby has been abducted from her bed. The childs nanny - the last person to see her - gives a garbled account of events, covering for her boyfriend who had visited on the night in question. But as Tony gently interrogates the parents, another suspect emerges

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads north to pick up a 1954 Morgan +4 coupe in mint condition and believed to be just one of three still on the road

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek waxes lyrical about a pristine Jaguar XJ8 and a Ford Model T van tests his driving skills

programme.title: A Very British Murder with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian tells the story of one of the first high-profile killers - Dr Crippen, who was hanged in 1910 for poisoning and dismembering his wife - before turning her attention to the interwar period, when detective fiction reached the peak of its popularity at the hands of authors Agatha Christie and Dorothy L Sayers. After undergoing the elaborate initiation ceremony of the Detection Club, which was set up by a group of British writers in 1930, Lucy considers how Alfred Hitchcocks films and Graham Greenes books eclipsed the traditional murder-mystery story in the depiction of homicide

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: The explorer digs out his rucksack and heads for Windermere in the Lake District. The National Park attracts 18 million visitors each year, and is now a World Heritage site. Here, Paul finds out why the early tourists thought the Lakes was one of the most dangerous spots in the country. He also plunges into the world of long-distance swimming and helps the staff who run the boats on the lake and meets a Holocaust survivor who was airlifted to Windermere in 1945 as part of a mercy mission involving 300 orphans after the Second World War

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: The explorer heads to Derwentwater and Borrowdale, a region of England susceptible to extreme weather, where he meets some of the locals who have battled against flooding and learned to live with the conditions. Along the way, Paul tells the story of Millican Dalton, the Professor of Adventure, who ran his own expeditions in the Lake District more than 70 years ago. He also heads for the summit of Great Gable, one of the Lakes best-known peaks

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Coniston Water isnt the biggest of Cumbrias lakes - but its one of the most iconic and the place where Donald Campbell died trying to break the world water speed record. Paul meets Campbells daughter Gina and crew member Robbie Robinson, who was there on the fateful day in January 1967 when Bluebird somersaulted and crashed on the water. The explorer also enters a Lakeland bike ride with a difference before ending his visit with a spot of rock climbing at Dow Crag before the ascent of one of the areas best known hills, The Old Man of Coniston

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Remote Eskdale is the next destination in the explorers trek through the Lake District, enjoying the landscapes and meeting its people. The corner of the western Lakes he focuses on is off the normal tourist track, yet boasts Englands highest mountain. However, before making the tough climb to the summit of Scafell Pike, Paul meets the folk who run one of the countrys most scenic railways, has a close encounter with a vulture, and gets to grips with the locals in a spot of Cumberland wrestling

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek travels to the Yorkshire coast to pick up a 1963 Austin J2 Paralanian campervan, described as the Rolls Royce of campervans.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: War Factories
programme.sub-title: The Story of the Machine Gun
programme.desc: Hiram Maxim developed the worlds first fully automatic machine gun, but it weighed a whopping 136lbs - until British company Vickers adapted the design

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: After the Nuremberg trial, the most senior ranking Nazis had been executed or imprisoned - but many of the worst criminals were still at large. They included Adolf Eichmann, who arranged the transportation of millions of Europes Jewish people to death camps, and Nazi secret police chief Klaus Barbie, who became known as the Butcher of Lyon because of his reputation for extreme cruelty. This documentary follows the hunt for these two war criminals

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Ham House in the London borough of Richmond-upon-Thames, where an 18th-century robe could be an important piece of Chinese history. Other items brought in include a portrait by Indian-born artist FN Souza, a collection of silver from the Gulf state of Oman, two goblets engraved with images of the Crystal Palace, a dressing gown made by from a blanket by a German prisoner of war, and a photo album that documents the moment British atomic bombs were tested in 1956

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Dyffryn Gardens near Cardiff, where treasures include a necklace made from some of the largest pieces of Baltic amber that jewellery specialist John Benjamin has ever seen. Jon Baddeley admires a humble compass once owned by local man Edgar Evans, who was part of Scotts ill-fated Antarctic expedition and one of the first of the party to lose his life on the ice

programme.desc: Derek Mathewson collects a Jaguar Mark 2 and explains why they made such poor getaway cars for 1960s bank robbers. A customer considers a Morris Marina as a Christmas present

programme.title: Wilderness with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels through some of the most remote landscapes on Earth. programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Letchworth Garden City to Herne Hill
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores an Edwardian utopia in Letchworth, where he meets a fourth generation citizen who introduces him to the towns community spirit. He then heads towards London, and discovers a favoured haunt of King Edward VII, before sampling the monarchs favourite tipple, the Kings Ginger

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Croydon, where he hears the story of a mixed-race composer who was once celebrated but is now forgotten. At Three Bridges station, he is delighted to find a recently built depot and its fleet of new Class 700 trains - and is given the great honour of washing one down. In Lewes, he makes a beeline for Charleston, the home of artists Vanessa Bell and Duncan Grant, and in Shoreham-by-Sea, Michael discovers how magnificent Edwardians in flying machines took off from the oldest licensed airport in the country

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to Chichester, West Sussex, where he encounters an Edwardian motoring duo patronised by King Edward VII. After a grand tour of their factory, he is treated to some rest and relaxation in one of their finest vehicles. Arriving in the resort of Southsea, Hampshire, Michael heads for the Kings Theatre, where he treads the boards in a community production of Lads in the Village. Crossing the Solent, the presenter boards the Island Line to travel along the pier in a 1930s London Underground carriage, then hops on to the Isle of Wight heritage line bound for Wootton

At the end of the challenge, which of the three competitors will she choose to take out on a date?

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen present the show in which famous faces cook up a storm week by week in a bid to outdo their rivals. Each of the celebrity contestants are paired with a professional chef who will mentor and teach them to prepare restaurant quality dishes. Anton Du Beke, Joe Wilkinson, Josie Gibson and Woody Cook are the first four contestants battling it out in the kitchen

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The concluding part of an interview with the Derry legend, who discusses his colourful football career and the place GAA has in society

programme.title: The Six OClock Show
programme.desc: A lively mix of celebrity chat with national and international guests, fabulous food from the country?s top Chefs, expert advice and all the unmissable stories making the headlines

programme.title: Gift Wrapped
programme.desc: Quiz show in which three engaged couples get the chance to win their wedding gift list and a dream holiday. The betrothed pairs work together across a series of rounds to see off their opponents. Hosted by Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the Olympic boxing silver medallist, who discusses his journey to the top of the amateur boxing world and his hidden struggles with addiction

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Biologist and extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to the heart of Africa journeying along the Congo, a river shrouded in mystery and steeped in legend. While exploring the tale of Mami Wata, a spirit that is said to lure fishermen from their boats to the bottom of the river, where they are imprisoned for eternity, he discovers the truth is every bit as frightening as the myth

programme.title: Inside Sydney Airport
programme.desc: The airport is thrown into jeopardy during a busy Christmas period with the discovery of an unidentified white powder on board an incoming flight

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade embarks on one of his most challenging journeys as he investigates reports of a face-eating creature in a remote part of Bolivia. Finding his skills and knowledge tested to the limit, he encounters one of his oldest adversaries and discovers how a species can adapt its behaviour to survive

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: In the final of the contest, the three remaining celebrities showcase all of the skills they have learnt as they compete against each other in a bid to be crowned the winner. Hosted by Emma Willis and Tom Allen

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: English teacher Val tries to beat the Chasers in order to replace her car, while Frankie from Dorset and Kieran from Romford attempt to fund overseas travels. Singer-songwriter Leah also has a pop at the brainboxes to fulfil her dream of creating a music video before Gary from Rugeley tries his luck so that he can follow Bruce Springsteen on his world tour. Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Haydn from Cheshire, Romanda from Solihull, Makyla from Buckinghamshire and Roger from Sutton Coldfield pit their wits against one of the Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught. Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Alan Quinlan: Red Blooded
programme.desc: Part one of two. Alan Quinlan talks to Joe Molloy about his Ireland career. The back-row forward won 27 international caps during a nine-year period full of ups and downs. In the first part, he discusses heartbreak in 2003, when dislocated his shoulder in the process of scoring against Argentina, an injury which ruled him out of the World Cup in Australia

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The concluding part of an interview with the Derry legend, who discusses his colourful football career and the place GAA has in society

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host this quiz contest, in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try and win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Paul OGradys Animal Orphans
programme.desc: In his final week in Africa, the comedian and presenter visits Bambelela, a rehabilitation centre for orphaned vervet monkeys, where he looks after 10-month-old Toledo for the day. Returning to Moholoholo Wildlife Rehabilitation Centre, Paul discovers what its like to be a volunteer, from the daily walk through the Bush, to cleaning the enclosures and encountering the smelliest animal at the facility, a honey badger called Julius. After that, he heads to Hoedspruit Endangered Species Centre to meet Little G, an orphaned rhino who has been given a permanent companion, a sheep named Lamby

programme.title: Animal Emergency
programme.desc: A French bulldog arrives at the vets for specialist spinal surgery to help him walk properly. Staff in the large animal department have to decide if a wild seals eye is able to be saved, and nurse Clodagh and vet Myles have to look long and hard to find a magnet in a parrots gizzard

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A teenage boy is brought into the Royal having fallen from a cliff, but it soon emerges that the incident was a suicide attempt as evidence of extreme depression comes to the surface. Dr Cheriton contacts the youngsters psychiatrist, but is taken aback when electroshock therapy is suggested as a form of treatment, so ventures to the family home to recommend an alternative - only for tragedy to strike. Meanwhile, a hypochondriac causes chaos at the hospital when she insists her dog be treated following a snake bite, and a heart-to-heart talk between Drs Weatherill and Ormerod leads to a passionate kiss. Honor Blackman guest stars

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A family hunting expedition ends in a youngsters tragic death, and Ken clashes with Harper over the hospital laundry. Meanwhile, staff at the Royal persuade a devout Catholic mother to be sterilised - much to the disgust of her priest. Drama, starring Robert Daws, Linda Armstrong and Michael Starke

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: A profile of the Gaelic football legend who grew up in The Troubles in Armagh, and recovered from a missed penalty to score the winning goal in their only All-Ireland win

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news stories, looking back at the headlines from 1998

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: A look back at the stories making the headlines in 1999, set to a soundtrack of music from the year

programme.title: Animal Emergency
programme.desc: Clare native Vincent is attending the animal hospital with Porsche, his once speedy greyhound that he fears is on her last legs. Meanwhile, making the trip from Wicklow is Kealan Doyle and his pet Kunekune pigs Hank and Joey, who are lined up for a badly needed operation. Sapphire, a Shibu Inu dog is brought to the Vet Hospital after a sudden decline in her health, but her medical needs come at a huge financial cost for owner Bernadette

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Tommy Martin interviews the former Republic of Ireland footballer, who reflects on a career that began in the League of Ireland, and saw him move to the top of the English game

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news stories from 2002, the year of Roy Keanes famous spat with Mick McCarthy, igniting a national debate that continues to this day. Bertie Ahern led Fianna Fail to victory in the general election but fell just short of an overall majority and a levy of 15 per cent was introduced on plastic bags

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Headline stories from 2003, accompanied by the music of the time

programme.title: UEFA Man in the Middle
programme.desc: A look behind the scenes as UEFA and its referees prepare for the final eight of the 2019/20 UEFA Champions League in Lisbon

programme.title: Inside Sydney Airport
programme.desc: Baggage Manager Kevin has a rough day trouble-shooting a number of mechanical breakdowns, while security screening discovers a passenger travelling with a gun

programme.title: Generation Dating
programme.desc: Two strangers with a substantial age gap are brought together, and each is challenged to find a suitable date for the other

programme.title: Kate Garraways Life Stories
programme.desc: Kate Garraway interviews Jeremy Kyle, best known to many for his explosive daytime TV series, discussing his early years, rise to fame and the subsequent fall from grace.

programme.title: The Real Housewives of Beverly Hills
programme.desc: Part two of three. The reunion continues as Sutton and Kyles issues come to a head and jeopardise their friendship like never before

programme.title: Below Deck Sailing Yacht
programme.desc: Parsifal IIIs new crew try to figure out how they can work together to execute a successful first charter. The first crew night out leads to conflict when Ashley dismisses Toms advances because of his age

programme.title: Vanderpump Rules
programme.desc: Lala drops a bombshell on Raquel that shakes the foundation of their newfound friendship. Sandoval takes some time away from the bar to play rock star with his cover band

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: A body is dredged up from the English Channel, setting in motion a truly disturbing investigation that forces Detective Superintendent Roy Grace to ask himself just how far someone might go to save the ones they love. Crime drama based on the books by Peter James, starring John Simm, with Richie Campbell, Rakie Ayola, Laura Elphinstone, Brad Morrison, Craig Parkinson and Zoe Tapper

programme.desc: A lively mix of celebrity chat with national and international guests, fabulous food from the country?s top Chefs, expert advice and all the unmissable stories making the headlines

programme.title: The Chase

programme.title: Time Is Money
programme.desc: Sara Davies hosts the quiz in which contestants are given their prize money before they have answered a question, and must race against time to keep hold of it. Each player stands before an hourglass-shaped money timer that begins to drain as the questions are asked - and the only way of stopping it is to answer correctly. Once the money is gone, the contestant is out - and only one can make the final to play for the amount they have managed to save

programme.title: Long Lost Family: Born Without Trace
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell return with more stories of people who were abandoned as babies, helping them try to find their birth families and know their identities. programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Janice persuades the pals to reveal their deepest, darkest secrets about a period in their relationships three years earlier. Phoebe is trying to move out without telling Monica, Rachel wants one last fling before she gets married and Ross makes a distressing discovery. Comedy, starring Matthew Perry and Maggie Wheeler

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Isaac is upset that his dad has cancer, while Cain is preoccupied in his search for farmland and Pollard tests Kerrys loyalty

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Daniel seeks answers from Megan about the future of their relationship, and Theo hatches a new plan, announcing that hes been offered a job in Belfast before spiking an oblivious Todds drink

programme.title: Betrayal
programme.desc: John is still reeling after his discovery that a proxy attack is possibly being planned on UK soil to target a prominent Iranian dissident, but a visit to an informants widow throws up contradictory evidence. John becomes convinced that there is a cover-up that permeates to the highest level. Meanwhile, Johns refusal to accept voluntary redundancy means his relationship with Claire is on thin ice, and a visit from his in-laws only stokes tensions. Thriller, starring Shaun Evans and Romola Garai

programme.title: Romesh Ranganathans Parents Evening
programme.desc: Comedy game show in which celebrities team up with family members, with the parents choosing which questions their children will answer and gambling on how many they can get right. The first edition features Match of the Day presenter Gabby Logan and her daughter Lois, reality TV star Pete Wicks and his mum Tracey and journalist Krishnan Guru-Murthy and his daughter Jasmine

programme.title: Gogglebox Ireland
programme.desc: Deirdre OKane provides the voiceover as a selection of telly addicts pass comment on the weeks programmes from the comfort of their living rooms. From December 2016

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a charity edition of the game show, with Ben Bailey Smith, Claire Richards and Lisa Riley all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen good causes

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi returns to Rosehill House, a 30-bedroom hotel in Burnley. When Alex first arrived two and a half years ago, she met a dad with a deadline and a daughter in despair. With the hotel badly underperforming, John was threatening to sell it and turn it into flats, but daughter Emma was unable to let it go. Have they managed to turn the business around?

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Staff at Hotel Benidorm Plaza are trying to keep their cool as the reception air conditioning has a temporary malfunction, while guest experience manager Rosanna sweeps Holly and Dan off to one of Benidorms best-kept secrets. Owner Fede and his wife are turning 40 and have challenged hotel manager Angel to throw them a party at the hotel for the great and the good of Benidorm.

programme.title: John and Lisas Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner have advice on baking, including a classic coffee and walnut cake, a traybake featuring fish, potatoes and croutons, and strawberry shortcake crunch

programme.title: Champions: Full Gallop
programme.desc: Documentary following the British horse-racing jump season, beginning as it bursts into action at Cheltenhams November meeting. Rival jockeys Harry Cobden, Nico de Boinville, Sean Bowen and Harry Skelton begin a battle in the all-new David Power Jockey Cup, a league format showdown with £500,000 in prize money up for grabs, with emotions running high as rising star Freddie Gingell steps into the spotlight

programme.title: The Floor
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the grand finale of the game show, with the remaining nine contestants facing one last series of high-stakes duels. Two players will compete in a best-of-three final, but only one of them will conquer the Floor and take home the £50,000 jackpot

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show which takes place in public locations around the UK. He invites passers-by in Newcastle to take part in a series of unusual challenges, with a prize of £1,000 up for grabs

programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the exciting and fast-paced quiz where each episode sees six hopefuls compete for £1 million - however, only one player can take their place in the Hot Seat at a time and answer multiple-choice questions to work their way up the Money Ladder

programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Cherry Healey, Dallas Campbell, Ria Hebden and Robert Peston pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.desc: A look back at the main local, national and international stories of the week

programme.title: News at 5.30
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories from the Virgin Media News team

programme.title: Rookie Nurses
programme.desc: The daily reality for rookie nurses - especially in AandE - as they deal with life-or-death situations and everything in between. Twenty-two-year-old Mikey is part of the trauma team tasked with helping a teenage rugby player brought in by ambulance with severe concussion in Carmarthen. In Aberystwyth, 24-year-old nurse is Leah is unphased when a patient arrives having suffered a freak lawnmower accident which led to an amputation. Amid medical dramas on the wards, theres a big moment for healthcare assistant Caitlin who, after years of studying, is on her way to collect her new nurse uniform, and is excited to join her colleagues in her blues

programme.title: Britains Got Talent
programme.desc: The auditions make their way to Birminghams Hippodrome Theatre, where guest judge Stacey Solomon - who knows a thing or two about auditioning in front of a live audience, after coming third on The X Factor in 2009 - joins the panel alongside KSI, Alesha Dixon and Amanda Holden

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.sub-title: David James, Lucrezia Millarini, Myles Stephenson and Joe Sugg
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as David James, Lucrezia Millarini, Myles Stephenson and Joe Suggn pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: The presenter is joined by comedian and award-winning podcaster Joanne McNally, who speaks to Claudia about her record-breaking stand-up tour, Pinotphile. Bafta-nominated comedian and actor Guz Khan speaks about his new Amazon Prime series Bait, which he stars in alongside Riz Ahmed. Former One Direction star and singer-songwriter Niall Horan joins Claudia to share how he went from global boy band success to solo chart-topping artist and talk about his new album, featuring his latest single Dinner Party

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Jeremy Clarkson hosts

programme.title: Andrew and Fergie: An Unusual Relationship
programme.desc: Documentary focused on the life of King Charles IIIs troubled younger brother, who has publicly fallen from grace. His former wife is the outspoken Duchess Sarah Ferguson, who apparently cant avoid trouble in the eyes of the media. Together they make up the most controversial duo in the British royal family

programme.title: Coma
programme.desc: Simon takes matters into his own hands, threatening his old boss Jimmy to fast-track his redundancy payment - otherwise he will reveal that Jimmy got rid of him as part of a personal vendetta. Meanwhile, DS Evans follows up a call Simon made the night before the attack, reporting a threatening local youth

programme.title: Billy Connolly: Journey to the Edge of the World
programme.desc: The comedian boards a Russian cruise ship to make an eight-day trip through the Northwest Passage and visit the sites where explorers have lived and died. Stopping off on Beechey Island, where Victorian naval officer John Franklin was stranded for two years, Billy moves on to Gjoa Haven, a settlement on King William Island where Roald Amundsen stayed 50 years after Franklins expedition. He then heads south to Dawson City, the site of a gold rush in the late 19th century
